breadcrumb.label: Buying a House in the UK – Step by Step

article.title: Buying a House in the UK – Step by Step Process
article.intro: A complete guide for first-time buyers. Understand the entire process from budgeting to getting the keys to your new home.

key_takeaways.title: 💡 Key Takeaways
items: Understand each step of the home buying journey
Know the costs involved at every stage
Get helpful tips from property experts

sections.heading: 1. Overview
sections.text: Buying a home is one of the biggest decisions you'll make. It can seem complicated, but breaking the journey down into clear steps makes it much easier. This guide walks you through each stage of the process.

items.title: Step 1: Check Your Finances & Budget
items.desc: Review your income, expenses, debts and savings. Understand how much you can afford including deposit and buying costs.

items.title: Step 2: Get a Mortgage Agreement in Principle
items.desc: A mortgage agreement in principle (AIP) shows how much you can borrow and strengthens your position when making an offer.

items.title: Step 3: Find a Property
items.desc: Search for properties online, go for viewings and shortlist the homes that best meet your needs.

items.title: Step 4: Make an Offer
items.desc: Once you find the right home, your offer is submitted. The seller can accept, reject or make a counter-offer.

items.title: Step 5: Survey & Valuation
items.desc: A survey checks the property's condition, while the lender arranges a valuation to confirm it's worth the loan.

items.title: Step 6: Conveyancing (Legal Process)
items.desc: Your solicitor handles the legal work, including searches, contracts and ensuring the property is legally yours.

items.title: Step 7: Exchange of Contracts
items.desc: Both sides sign the legal documents and pay the deposit. The sale is now legally binding.

items.title: Step 8: Completion Day
items.desc: The remaining money is transferred and you receive the keys to your new home. Congratulations!

tip.text: The full process usually takes 8–12 weeks on average, but it can be shorter or longer depending on your circumstances.

sections.heading: 3. Costs You Should Know
sections.text: When buying a home, there are several costs to consider beyond the purchase price.

author.name: ADVAITH HOMES Team
author.disclaimer: The information on this website is general guidance only and does not constitute legal, financial or professional advice. Please seek expert advice for your specific situation.
